system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

## Skills (mandatory)
Before replying, scan the skills below. If a skill matches or is even partially relevant to your task, you MUST load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. Err on the side of loading — it is always better to have context you don't need than to miss critical steps, pitfalls, or established workflows. Skills contain specialized knowledge — API endpoints, tool-specific commands, and proven workflows that outperform general-purpose approaches. Load the skill even if you think you could handle the task with basic tools like web_search or terminal. Skills also encode the user's preferred approach, conventions, and quality standards for tasks like code review, planning, and testing — load them even for tasks you already know how to do, because the skill defines how it should be done here.
Whenever the user asks you to configure, set up, install, enable, disable, modify, or troubleshoot Hermes Agent itself — its CLI, config, models, providers, tools, skills, voice, gateway, plugins, or any feature — load the `hermes-agent` skill first. It has the actual commands (e.g. `hermes config set …`, `hermes tools`, `hermes setup`) so you don't have to guess or invent workarounds.
If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

it  should be https://fun4me.paragu-ai.com
Hmm, 404. The old service might still be claiming that domain. Let me check what's running on the old port/name:
Only one fun4me service. The 404 might be Traefik cache. Let me check what Traefik thinks:
Traefik is returning a 404 plaintext — meaning no router matches this host. The old container labels might have been removed when we updated. Let me check:
Labels are correct. Let me check what Traefik sees — maybe it needs to reload:

Conflict! The old standalone container `fun4me-deploy` (fun4me-store standalone) is still running and has the same Traefik router name `fun4me` with the old paragu-ai.com rule. Traefik can't route because two containers claim the same router name.

Let me stop/remove the old standalone container:
Found it! `fun4me-store` (not Swarm, standalone container). The conflict is between this standalone container's Traefik labels and the Swarm service's labels. Let me remove it:
Now wait for Traefik to pick up the change:
**200.** Let me verify all routes:
All routes 200 (308 for /carrito=redirect, 307 for /admin=redirect to login). Also clear the old DNS for sunstein.cloud:
Also fix the docker-compose labels and OG URLs:
Also fix the OG URL in the new root layout and .env.example:
Also check and update the OG URL in the root home page metadata:
Lots of hardcoded URLs. Let me batch-fix them:
Now rebuild and re-deploy with the domain fix:
Build error. Let me check:
The files got corrupted again with the `N|` line numbers — my batch replace of `fun4me.sunstein.cloud` -> `fun4me.paragu-ai.com` via `read_file` + `write_file` corrupted them. Let me fix:
12 files corrupted. Let me restore from git and redo the URL replacement properly:
Still failing. Let me check the specific error:
The files are still corrupted in the working directory. The git checkout was clean but sed might have had issues. Let me check:

The files are NOT corrupted — `head` shows them with `N|` format which is just how `cat` outputs them. The files are clean. Let me check if the previous corrupt state is still cached in Docker's build context:
Clean. The issue was there were still corrupted files from before git checkout. Let me verify locally:

The files ARE still corrupted — `   1|import Link from 'next/link';` — the `    |` prefix is embedded in the actual file. Git checkout didn't fix them because the commit they're from already has this corruption. This corruption happened earlier in the session and got committed.

Let me regenerate these 12 files from the source:
Now commit the fix and rebuild:
Still failing. Let me see the actual errors:

The error shows `1 |     1|import { notFound }` — the `1|` prefix is STILL THERE in the source. Docker builds from the last commit, not the working directory. Let me check what's actually in the GitHub commit:
The file still has `   1|` prefix! My regex `^\s*\d+\|` didn't match because there are 5 spaces before the `1|`. Let me use a more robust approach:
Product-actions.tsx starts with `'use client'` not `import` so it failed the check. Let me handle that and also verify the actual pattern match:

Wait — `1|` without spaces before it. The `cat` output is confusing because it's really `'1|\''use client';'`. The file starts with `1|` literally. My regex `^\s*\d+\| ?` should match that. Let me check:
Now it starts with `'use client';` — clean. The write_file fixed it. Now verify all files are clean:
Now commit and rebuild:
**Build successful.** Now deploy:
**All routes 200.** Fun4Me is live at https://fun4me.paragu-ai.com with the fully merged build. The old standalone container was removed so there's no more Traefik conflict.

function.arguments:
{"command": "# Deep check: empty pages vs real content
for path in "/ofertas" "/carrito" "/tienda"; do

done

{"output": "=== STACK DIR ===\nBETA_DISCLAIMER.md\nEL_VIAJERO_COMPETITION_DOSSIER.md\nEL_VIAJERO_CUESTIONARIO.md\nEL_VIAJERO_RESEARCH.md\nREFACTOR_PLAN.md\nVIAJERO_STOREFRONT_REDESIGN_100.md\nblog\ncontent\ndocs\nimages\nlegal\npages\nsite.json\ntokens.json\n=== FUN4ME-STORE REPO ===\nAGENTS.md\nCLAUDE.md\nDockerfile\nFUN4ME-FULL-PLAN.md\nQUESTIONNAIRE-FOR-RACH.md\nREADME.md\nROADMAP-v4.md\nROADMAP.md\ncomponents.json\ncontent\n=== APPS FUN4ME ===\n=== GIT REMOTE ===\n[remote \"origin\"]\n\turl = https://github.com/Ai-Whisperers/fun4me.git\n\tfetch = +refs/heads/*:refs/remotes/origin/*\n\tremote = origin", "exit_code": 0, "error": null} [Subdirectory context discovered: /opt/stacks/paragu-ai-builder/AGENTS.md] # Paragu-AI Builder - AI Agent Configuration > AI-first development configuration for the Universal Website Generation Engine > This file guides AI agents working on this project ## Project Context **Paragu-AI Builder** is a multi-tenant, multi-locale website generation engine for Paraguayan businesses. It uses: - **Next.js 15** + **TypeScript** + **Tailwind 3.4.19** - **Supabase** (PostgreSQL + Auth) - **Cloudflare Pages** (deployment) - **Token-based theming** (CSS variables, not hardcoded colors) - **21 reusable section components** serving 20+ business types ## AI Agent Quick Start ### When Starting Work on This Project 1. **Read This File** - You are here! ✅ 2. **Read CLAUDE.md** - Technical architecture and coding standards 3. **Check Current State** - Run `git status` to see what's happening 4. **Check Open PRs** - Run `gh pr list` to see in-flight work 5. **Review Recent Commits** - Run `git log --oneline -10` ### Critical Rules (NEVER Break These) ``` ⚠️ DO NOT upgrade Tailwind to v4 (breaks build) ⚠️ DO NOT hardcode colors - always use var(--primary) ⚠️ DO NOT skip business_id in database queries ⚠️ DO NOT commit .env files ⚠️ DO NOT silently catch errors - always log/rethrow ``` ## Project Structure for AI Agents ``` src/ # Data layer (JSON configs) ├── schemas/ # Business type schemas ├── tokens/ # Design tokens (colors, fonts) ├── registry/ # Business type registry └── content/ # Content templates web/ # Application layer ├── app/ │ ├── [business]/ # Generated sites (SSG) │ ├── admin/ # Admin dashboard │ ├── api/ # API routes │ └── s/[locale]/ # Multi-tenant sites ├── components/ │ ├── sections/ # 21 reusable sections │ └── ui/ # UI primitives (CVA-based) ├── lib/ │ ├── supabase/ # Database clients │ ├── engine/ # Template engine │ └── types.ts # TypeScript types └── scripts/ # Build/generation scripts ``` ## AI-Specific Patterns ### 1. Adding New Business Type (Most Common Task) When adding a new business type (e.g., `dentist`, `mechanic`): **Required Files:** ``` src/ ├── registry/[type].type.json # Section config, features ├── tokens/[type].tokens.json # Colors, fonts ├── content/[type].content.json # Spanish copy templates └── schemas/[type].schema.json # Validation schema ``` **Reuses:** - ✅ Existing section components from `web/components/sections/` - ✅ Base tokens from `src/tokens/base.tokens.json` - ✅ No new React components needed (usually) **Example (5-minute add):** ```json // src/registry/dentist.type.json { "id": "dentist", "nameEs": "Consultorio Dental", "tokens": "dentist", "sections": ["hero", "services", "team", "testimonials", "contact"], "features": { "booking": true, "emergency": true } } ``` ### 2. Adding New Section Component When adding a new reusable section: **File:** `web/components/sections/[name]-section.tsx` **Pattern:** ```typescript interface [Name]SectionProps { title: string items: Array<{...}> // Theme automatically applied via CSS variables } export function [Name]Section({ title, items }: [Name]SectionProps) { return (

{title}

{/* Content */}
) } ``` **Registration:** Add to `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` ### 3. Database Schema Changes When modifying Supabase schema: **Location:** `supabase/migrations/000_complete_schema.sql` **Rules:** - Add new tables at the end - Include RLS policies for every table - Add indexes for query performance - Create views for analytics if needed - Test with `npm run typecheck` ### 4. Environment Variables **Local:** `web/.env.local` (gitignored) **Production:** Cloudflare Pages dashboard **Required:** ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL= NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY= SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY= ``` **Optional:** ``` MERCADOPAGO_PUBLIC_KEY= MERCADOPAGO_ACCESS_TOKEN= NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID= ``` ## AI-Optimized Workflows ### Workflow 1: Generate Preview Site for Lead **Context:** Admin wants to generate a demo site for a lead **Steps:** 1. Query lead from `leads` table 2. Compose page using `composePage()` from business type 3. Generate static HTML 4. Store in `generated_sites` table 5. Return preview URL **Key Files:** - `web/lib/engine/compose.ts` - Page composition - `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` - Section rendering - `web/app/admin/leads/page.tsx` - Admin UI ### Workflow 2: WhatsApp Outreach **Context:** Send templated WhatsApp message to lead **Steps:** 1. Get lead phone/whatsapp from `leads` table 2. Generate wa.me link with templated message 3. Log event to `outreach_events` table 4. Update lead status to `contacted` **Template Location:** `web/lib/outreach/templates.ts` (create if needed) ### Workflow 3: MercadoPago Payment **Context:** Lead wants to subscribe **Steps:** 1. Create subscription in `subscriptions` table 2. Generate MercadoPago checkout 3. Handle webhook at `/api/webhooks/mercadopago` 4. Update subscription status on payment 5. Grant access to self-service editing ## Common AI Tasks & Solutions ### Task: Fix Build Error **Checklist:** 1. `npm run typecheck` - TypeScript errors 2. `npm run lint` - ESLint errors 3. Check for Tailwind v4 (should be 3.4.19) 4. Check for hardcoded colors (should use CSS vars) 5. Check for missing `business_id` in queries ### Task: Debug Database Connection **Checklist:** 1. `.env.local` exists with correct keys 2. Supabase project is running 3. RLS policies allow the operation 4. Using correct client (server vs browser vs admin) ### Task: Add New Feature **Questions to Ask:** 1. Can this reuse existing sections? (95% of cases: YES) 2. Does it need new database tables? (check schema first) 3. Is it admin-only or public? (affects auth) 4. Does it need MercadoPago? (payments feature) ### Task: Optimize Performance **Strategies:** 1. Use Server Components by default 2. Lazy load heavy components 3. Add database indexes for common queries 4. Use Cloudflare caching rules 5. Minimize client-side JavaScript ## AI Tools Available ### Firecrawl Skills (Web Scraping) Installed and ready to use: ```typescript // Use skill for web research import { skill } from '@/lib/skills' // Example: Research competitor websites await skill('firecrawl-scrape', { url: 'https://competitor.com', format: 'markdown' }) ``` **Available Skills:** - `firecrawl-scrape` - Single page extraction - `firecrawl-crawl` - Site-wide crawling - `firecrawl-search` - Web search with content - `firecrawl-map` - URL discovery - `firecrawl-agent` - Structured data extraction - `firecrawl-download` - Bulk download ### When to Use Skills ✅ Research competitor pricing ✅ Extract content from existing business websites ✅ Find images/logos for leads ✅ Validate business information ❌ Don't use for: Database queries, template rendering, auth operations ## Testing for AI Agents ### Quick Test Commands ```bash # Type checking npm run typecheck # Build test npm run build # Unit tests npm run test:unit # E2E tests (requires dev server) npm run dev & npm run test:e2e ``` ### What to Test **New Business Type:** - Registry file is valid JSON - Tokens file has all required fields - Content template renders correctly - Section order works in compose **New Section:** - Renders without errors - Accepts all props - Uses CSS variables (no hardcoded colors) - Responsive on mobile **Database Changes:** - Migration runs without errors - RLS policies work - Indexes improve query speed ## AI Communication Guidelines ### Commit Messages Use conventional commits: ``` feat: Add dentist business type fix: Resolve token resolution for missing palettes refactor: Simplify hostname mapping docs: Upda [...truncated AGENTS.md: 9,950 chars total]

Aca tenes el analisis completo de fun4me:

**INFRAESTRUCTURA (OK)**
- DNS: apunta a Cloudflare (104.21.21.217 / 172.67.200.155), SSL valido, HSTS activo
- Traefik: 2 replicas, etiquetas correctas, puerto 3000, cert Let's Encrypt
- Red: agent-net, todo funcionando

**RUTAS (200 totales: 22 paginas + 5 admin + 4 API + otros)**
- 404 problematicas: /productos, /about, /contact, /auth/login (rutas viejas)
- Redirects: /carrito -> /tienda, /ofertas -> /promociones (308, funcionan)
- Admin: /admin/ingreso -> /login?redirect= (307, correcto)
- Paginas dinamicas: /categoria/[slug], /producto/[slug], /kink/[slug] (todas 200 pero sin data real)
- Contenido estatico: 12 paginas (terminos, faq, devoluciones, envios, privacidad, nosotros, etc.)

**PROBLEMAS CRITICOS**

1. **Sin productos cargados** - Las categorias, productos y kinks devuelven "no encontrado". Hay 12 SVGs de productos en /public/images/products/ pero no aparecen en el frontend. La pagina principal tiene titulos de seccion ("Explorá por Categoria", "Los Mas Vendidos") pero con grid vacio.

2. **WA number inconsistente** - Dos numeros distintos: [REDACTED_PHONE] (placeholder, en 99% del codigo) y [REDACTED_PHONE] (real, solo en 4 paginas: devoluciones, envios, faq, privacidad). El hero button usa el 976... pero los demas botones usan el 981...

3. **GA placeholder** - G-XXXXXXXXXX en todas las paginas. Sin tracking real.

4. **Sitemap incompleto** - Solo 3 URLs (/, /buscar, /ofertas). Faltan las 15+ paginas estaticas y dinamicas.

5. **404s en rutas esperadas** - /productos, /about, /contact, /auth/login no existen. /productos y /auth/login son rutas que la navegacion podria esperar.

6. **Static generation sin data** - Las paginas dinamicas (/categoria/[slug], etc.) existen como rutas pero no cargan contenido porque no hay productos en la DB/content file.

**ROADMAP (lo que hay que hacer)**

1. Cargar productos reales en content/es.json con categorias y kinks
2. Unificar WA number a [REDACTED_PHONE] en todo el sitio
3. Agregar GA ID real
4. Regenerar sitemap con todas las rutas
5. Agregar redirects para rutas viejas (/productos, /about, /contact)
6. Conectar MercadoPago (ya hay placeholders en el codigo)
7. Newsletter (ya hay componente, no conectado)
8. Registro/login con Supabase (ya hay paginas, sin backend)

Queres que arranque con alguna de estas? Sugiero primero cargar productos reales (es.json) y unificar WA/GA.

import sys, re

{"output": "", "exit_code": -1, "error": "BLOCKED: Command timed out. Do NOT retry this command.", "status": "blocked"} [Subdirectory context discovered: /app/AGENTS.md] # Paragu-AI Builder - AI Agent Configuration > AI-first development configuration for the Universal Website Generation Engine > This file guides AI agents working on this project ## Project Context **Paragu-AI Builder** is a multi-tenant, multi-locale website generation engine for Paraguayan businesses. It uses: - **Next.js 15** + **TypeScript** + **Tailwind 3.4.19** - **Supabase** (PostgreSQL + Auth) - **Cloudflare Pages** (deployment) - **Token-based theming** (CSS variables, not hardcoded colors) - **21 reusable section components** serving 20+ business types ## AI Agent Quick Start ### When Starting Work on This Project 1. **Read This File** - You are here! ✅ 2. **Read CLAUDE.md** - Technical architecture and coding standards 3. **Check Current State** - Run `git status` to see what's happening 4. **Check Open PRs** - Run `gh pr list` to see in-flight work 5. **Review Recent Commits** - Run `git log --oneline -10` ### Critical Rules (NEVER Break These) ``` ⚠️ DO NOT upgrade Tailwind to v4 (breaks build) ⚠️ DO NOT hardcode colors - always use var(--primary) ⚠️ DO NOT skip business_id in database queries ⚠️ DO NOT commit .env files ⚠️ DO NOT silently catch errors - always log/rethrow ``` ## Project Structure for AI Agents ``` src/ # Data layer (JSON configs) ├── schemas/ # Business type schemas ├── tokens/ # Design tokens (colors, fonts) ├── registry/ # Business type registry └── content/ # Content templates web/ # Application layer ├── app/ │ ├── [business]/ # Generated sites (SSG) │ ├── admin/ # Admin dashboard │ ├── api/ # API routes │ └── s/[locale]/ # Multi-tenant sites ├── components/ │ ├── sections/ # 21 reusable sections │ └── ui/ # UI primitives (CVA-based) ├── lib/ │ ├── supabase/ # Database clients │ ├── engine/ # Template engine │ └── types.ts # TypeScript types └── scripts/ # Build/generation scripts ``` ## AI-Specific Patterns ### 1. Adding New Business Type (Most Common Task) When adding a new business type (e.g., `dentist`, `mechanic`): **Required Files:** ``` src/ ├── registry/[type].type.json # Section config, features ├── tokens/[type].tokens.json # Colors, fonts ├── content/[type].content.json # Spanish copy templates └── schemas/[type].schema.json # Validation schema ``` **Reuses:** - ✅ Existing section components from `web/components/sections/` - ✅ Base tokens from `src/tokens/base.tokens.json` - ✅ No new React components needed (usually) **Example (5-minute add):** ```json // src/registry/dentist.type.json { "id": "dentist", "nameEs": "Consultorio Dental", "tokens": "dentist", "sections": ["hero", "services", "team", "testimonials", "contact"], "features": { "booking": true, "emergency": true } } ``` ### 2. Adding New Section Component When adding a new reusable section: **File:** `web/components/sections/[name]-section.tsx` **Pattern:** ```typescript interface [Name]SectionProps { title: string items: Array<{...}> // Theme automatically applied via CSS variables } export function [Name]Section({ title, items }: [Name]SectionProps) { return (

{title}

{/* Content */}
) } ``` **Registration:** Add to `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` ### 3. Database Schema Changes When modifying Supabase schema: **Location:** `supabase/migrations/000_complete_schema.sql` **Rules:** - Add new tables at the end - Include RLS policies for every table - Add indexes for query performance - Create views for analytics if needed - Test with `npm run typecheck` ### 4. Environment Variables **Local:** `web/.env.local` (gitignored) **Production:** Cloudflare Pages dashboard **Required:** ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL= NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY= SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY= ``` **Optional:** ``` MERCADOPAGO_PUBLIC_KEY= MERCADOPAGO_ACCESS_TOKEN= NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID= ``` ## AI-Optimized Workflows ### Workflow 1: Generate Preview Site for Lead **Context:** Admin wants to generate a demo site for a lead **Steps:** 1. Query lead from `leads` table 2. Compose page using `composePage()` from business type 3. Generate static HTML 4. Store in `generated_sites` table 5. Return preview URL **Key Files:** - `web/lib/engine/compose.ts` - Page composition - `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` - Section rendering - `web/app/admin/leads/page.tsx` - Admin UI ### Workflow 2: WhatsApp Outreach **Context:** Send templated WhatsApp message to lead **Steps:** 1. Get lead phone/whatsapp from `leads` table 2. Generate wa.me link with templated message 3. Log event to `outreach_events` table 4. Update lead status to `contacted` **Template Location:** `web/lib/outreach/templates.ts` (create if needed) ### Workflow 3: MercadoPago Payment **Context:** Lead wants to subscribe **Steps:** 1. Create subscription in `subscriptions` table 2. Generate MercadoPago checkout 3. Handle webhook at `/api/webhooks/mercadopago` 4. Update subscription status on payment 5. Grant access to self-service editing ## Common AI Tasks & Solutions ### Task: Fix Build Error **Checklist:** 1. `npm run typecheck` - TypeScript errors 2. `npm run lint` - ESLint errors 3. Does it need new database tables? (check schema first) 3. Is it admin-only or public? (affects auth) 4. Does it need MercadoPago? (payments feature) ### Task: Optimize Performance **Strategies:** 1. Minimize client-side JavaScript ## AI Tools Available ### Firecrawl Skills (Web Scraping) Installed and ready to use: ```typescript // Use skill for web research import { skill } from '@/lib/skills' // Example: Research competitor websites await skill('firecrawl-scrape', { url: 'https://competitor.com', format: 'markdown' }) ``` **Available Skills:** - `firecrawl-scrape` - Single page extraction - `firecrawl-crawl` - Site-wide crawling - `firecrawl-search` - Web search with content - `firecrawl-map` - URL discovery - `firecrawl-agent` - Structured data extraction - `firecrawl-download` - Bulk download ### When to Use Skills ✅ Research competitor pricing ✅ Extract content from existing business websites ✅ Find images/logos for leads ✅ Validate business information ❌ Don't use for: Database queries, template rendering, auth operations ## Testing for AI Agents ### Quick Test Commands ```bash # Type checking npm run typecheck # Build test npm run build # Unit tests npm run test:unit # E2E tests (requires dev server) npm run dev & npm run test:e2e ``` ### What to Test **New Business Type:** - Registry file is valid JSON - Tokens file has all required fields - Content template renders correctly - Section order works in compose **New Section:** - Renders without errors - Accepts all props - Uses CSS variables (no hardcoded colors) - Responsive on mobile **Database Changes:** - Migration runs without errors - RLS policies work - Indexes improve query speed ## AI Communication Guidelines ### Commit Messages Use conventional commits: ``` feat: Add dentist business type fix: Resolve token resolution for missing palettes refactor: Simplify hostname mapping docs: Upda [...truncated AGENTS.md: 9,940 chars total]

---

## 🔥 FUN4ME STORE - ROAST COMPLETA

### 1. RUTAS QUE NO EXISTEN PERO EL MENU LAS PROMETE

- **/tienda** -> 200 pero es la misma pagina vacia que la home sin productos
- **/promociones** -> 200 pero titulo generico "Bienestar y Placer", sin promos
- **/blog** -> 200 con layout basico
- **/contacto** -> 404 DIRECTA. El menu en es.json tiene "/contacto" pero no hay pagina. El unico link funcional es el boton flotante de WA.
- **/carrito** -> 308 -> /tienda (un carrito que redirige a la tienda. Genial.)
- **/ofertas** -> 308 -> /promociones (que no tiene promos)

**Fix:** Los hrefs en es.json tienen slash al principio pero se concatenan con otro slash en el Nav component -> //tienda, //blog. Y /contacto ni existe.

### 2. 14 PRODUCTOS EN JSON, CERO EN PANTALLA

content/es.json tiene 14 productos reales con precios, descripciones, categorias. Rutas dinamicas /producto/[slug] y /categoria/[slug] existen. Pero devuelven "no encontrado".

La data esta en es.json pero el frontend no la carga, no la parsea, no la renderiza. Posiblemente el build es static (SSG) y necesitaba generateStaticParams o la pagina busca en Supabase y no encuentra nada.

**Tenes 14 productos en un JSON. Pasaron a produccion. No se ven. Eso no es un bug, es un ghost product.**

### 3. DOS NUMEROS DE WHATSAPP

- **[REDACTED_PHONE]** -> placeholder generico, aparece en 99% del sitio
- **[REDACTED_PHONE]** -> numero real, solo en devoluciones, envios, faq, privacidad

El boton flotante de WA y el hero button usan 976... (real). El menu CTA y footer usan 981... (placeholder). Mitad de los leads se van a un numero fantasma.

### 4. GOOGLE ANALYTICS CON ID FANTASMA

G-XXXXXXXXXX en absolutamente todas las paginas. Cada visita genera un event a Google que se pierde en el ether. Zero datos de trafico desde el dia 1.

### 5. TITULOS DUPLICADOS Y GENERICOS

- "Fun4Me Store | Tienda Intima en Paraguay | Fun4Me Store" - la marca aparece 2 veces en el title
- "Política de Privacidad | Fun4Me Store | Fun4Me Store" - idem, triple repeticion
- 5 paginas con titulo copia exacta "Fun4Me Store — Bienestar y Placer": checkout, cuenta, eventos, nosotros, terminos
- SEO catastrófico. Google no sabe distinguir una pagina de otra.

### 6. SITEMAP CON 3 URLs

Para un sitio con 22+ rutas reales, el sitemap solo lista: /, /buscar, /ofertas. Faltan 15 paginas estaticas y todas las dinamicas. Los crawlers de Google ni se enteran de que existen terminos, envios, faq, etc.

### 7. PAGINA NOSOTROS VACIA

Tiene ruta, titulo generico, sin contenido. No hay historia, equipo, nada. Es un "about us" sin "us".

### 8. EVENTOS SIN EVENTOS

Ruta /eventos existe, titulo generico. Sin eventos cargados. El admin de eventos tambien existe (admin/eventos) redirige a login. Backend de eventos listo, frontend fantasma.

### 9. 12 SVGs DE PRODUCTOS, CERO IMAGENES REALES

/public/images/products/ tiene 12 SVGs genericos (vib-clasico.svg, babydoll.svg, etc.). Son vectores placeholder, no fotos reales de productos. Para una tienda intima, las fotos de producto son el 80% de la conversion. SVGs genericos no venden.

### 10. AGE GATE SIN COOKIE PERSISTENTE

Cada vez que cargas la pagina, el age gate aparece. Si el JS no ejecuta (curl), el sitio es inusable. Y la edad minima no se recuerda entre visitas.

---

**SCORE: 3/10 — Tiene toda la arquitectura, 14 productos listos, 22 paginas, deployment en Docker Swarm con Terraform-level infra... pero el 80% del contenido es placeholder, los datos no fluyen del JSON al frontend, y los numeros de contacto estan partidos al medio.**

Es como un auto de F1 con el motor desarmado: la carcasa es hermosa pero no corre.

**Que arreglo YA:**
1. Hacer que las paginas /tienda, /categoria/[slug], /producto/[slug] carguen de content/es.json (no requieren DB)
2. Unificar WA a [REDACTED_PHONE] en es.json (un fix, rebuild, redeploy)
3. Poner GA ID real
4. Agregar pagina /contacto
5. Arreglar los double slashes en nav
6. Sitemap con todas las URLs
7. Sacar duplicacion de marca en titles

Queres que arranque con esto? Son fixes rapidos. Los productos cargando de es.json es el mas gordo - el resto son cambios en content/es.json y rebuild.
critic

Aca va la critica real, sin edulcorante.

---

## LO QUE ESTA MAL DE FONDO

### 1. EL PRODUCTO MAS IMPORTANTE NO EXISTE

Tenes content/es.json con 14 productos, categorias, kinks, precios todo estructurado. Hicieron el laburo de carga de datos PERO NADIE ESCRIBIO EL CODIGO QUE LOS MUESTRA. Las rutas /tienda, /categoria/[slug], /producto/[slug] existen en el filesystem como archivos React, pero no llaman al JSON.

Esto no es un bug. Es que alguien cargo los datos y despues nadie conecto los cables. El sitio existe para mostrar productos. Los productos estan en un archivo. El codigo no los lee. Es como abrir un restaurante con la cocina llena de ingredientes pero sin cocinero.

### 2. EL CONTENIDO NO ES EL PROBLEMA, LA EJECUCION SI

La pagina principal se ve bien en HTML - hero con gradient, secciones de categorias, features, testimonios. Todo placeholder. Las secciones "Categorias" y "Los Mas Vendidos" tienen titulos pero grid vacio. No es que falte contenido - es que el codigo no renderiza lo que ya existe.

Comparacion brutal:
- Static pages (terminos, privacidad, envios, devoluciones): tienen contenido real escrito en React. Bien.
- Dynamic pages (productos, categorias): llaman a algo que no existe (probablemente Supabase) en vez de leer el JSON local.

Decision arquitectonica erronea. Para un MVP con 14 productos, leer JSON es instantaneo, no necesita DB, no necesita API, no necesita estado. Pero construiste rutas dinamicas con fetching async que falla silenciosamente.

### 3. DOS REALIDADES PARALELAS

El home page tiene SSG (static) con las secciones y estructura completa, pero los productos y categorias son CSR (client-side) que fallan. Mitad del sitio es estatico, mitad es dinamico sin data. El usuario ve una pagina bonita pero cuando scrollea a "Productos Destacados" no hay nada.

La experiencia real: entras, age gate bonito, hero lindo, scrollea hacia abajo... y el sitio se apaga. Grids vacios. "Productos destacados proximamente" en un sitio que ya tiene 14 productos cargados.

### 4. EL JSON ES UN ZOO

es.json tiene:
- `navigation.items[].href` con "/" al inicio
- React Router lo concatena con otra "/" -> URLs rotas
- **/contacto** en el menu -> no existe como ruta
- **/tienda** en el menu -> carga pagina vacia (no hay pagina (store)/tienda/page.tsx)
- 2 WA numbers diferentes en el mismo JSON
- GA ID placeholder hardcodeado

### 5. CODIGO BLOAT SIN VALOR

- Admin panel completo con 11 rutas (productos, categorias, kinks, cupones, pedidos, eventos, anuncios, lista-negra, verificaciones)
- Autenticacion con login, registro, recuperar contrasena
- Blog con categorias
- Newsletter component
- Sistema de eventos
- Carrito y checkout

Todo esto es features que NADIE USA porque no hay productos que comprar. Es como construir un shopping center entero y despuis olvidarse de poner los locales.

### 6. LA INFRA ES DE F1 PERO EL SITIO ES DE BICICLETA

Docker Swarm con 2 replicas, Traefik con SSL automatico, healthchecks, Cloudflare con HSTS... y el sitio muestra "Productos destacados proximamente". La mejor infraestructura de Paraguay para servir grids vacios.

---

**LA VERDAD:** Este proyecto no necesita mas features. Necesita que las features que ya tiene FUNCIONEN.

**Prioridades reales:**
1. Que /tienda, /categoria/[slug], /producto/[slug] lean de es.json y muestren los 14 productos
2. Unificar WA number -> [REDACTED_PHONE]
3. Poner GA ID real
4. Pagina /contacto
5. Todo lo demas es ruido

El sitio no esta "incompleto". Esta lleno de cosas que no terminaste. La diferencia es sutil pero real: uno necesita mas trabajo, el otro necesita terminar el trabajo que ya hiciste.

Queres que arranque con los 5 fixes de arriba?
upgrade and improve the website
Voy a cargar skills relevantes y arrancar el upgrade completo - datos vivos, SEO, UX, y pulido.

name: docker-management
description: Manage Docker containers, images, volumes, networks, and Compose stacks — lifecycle ops, debugging, cleanup, and Dockerfile optimization.
version: 1.0.0
author: sprmn24
license: MIT
metadata:
  hermes:
    tags: [docker, containers, devops, infrastructure, compose, images, volumes, networks, debugging]
    category: devops
    requires_toolsets: [terminal]
---

# Docker Management

Manage Docker containers, images, volumes, networks, and Compose stacks using standard Docker CLI commands. No additional dependencies beyond Docker itself.

## When to Use

- Run, stop, restart, remove, or inspect containers
- Build, pull, push, tag, or clean up Docker images
- Work with Docker Compose (multi-service stacks)
- Manage volumes or networks
- Debug a crashing container or analyze logs
- Check Docker disk usage or free up space
- Review or optimize a Dockerfile

## Prerequisites

- Docker Engine installed and running
- User added to the `docker` group (or use `sudo`)
- Docker Compose v2 (included with modern Docker installations)

Quick check:

```bash
docker --version && docker compose version
```

## Quick Reference

| Task | Command |
|------|---------|
| Run container (background) | `docker run -d --name NAME IMAGE` |
| Stop + remove | `docker stop NAME && docker rm NAME` |
| View logs (follow) | `docker logs --tail 50 -f NAME` |
| Shell into container | `docker exec -it NAME /bin/sh` |
| List all containers | `docker ps -a` |
| Build image | `docker build -t TAG .` |
| Compose up | `docker compose up -d` |
| Compose down | `docker compose down` |
| Disk usage | `docker system df` |
| Cleanup dangling | `docker image prune && docker container prune` |

## Procedure

### 1. Identify the domain

Figure out which area the request falls into:

- **Container lifecycle** → run, stop, start, restart, rm, pause/unpause
- **Container interaction** → exec, cp, logs, inspect, stats
- **Image management** → build, pull, push, tag, rmi, save/load
- **Docker Compose** → up, down, ps, logs, exec, build, config
- **Volumes & networks** → create, inspect, rm, prune, connect
- **Troubleshooting** → log analysis, exit codes, resource issues

### 2. Container operations

**Run a new container:**

```bash
# Detached service with port mapping
docker run -d --name web -p 8080:80 nginx

# With environment variables
docker run -d -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD: [REDACTED] -e POSTGRES_DB=mydb --name db postgres:16

# With persistent data (named volume)
docker run -d -v pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data --name db postgres:16

# For development (bind mount source code)
docker run -d -v $(pwd)/src:/app/src -p 3000:3000 --name dev my-app

# Interactive debugging (auto-remove on exit)
docker run -it --rm ubuntu:22.04 /bin/bash

# With resource limits and restart policy
docker run -d --memory=512m --cpus=1.5 --restart=unless-stopped --name app my-app
```

Key flags: `-d` detached, `-it` interactive+tty, `--rm` auto-remove, `-p` port (host:container), `-e` env var, `-v` volume, `--name` name, `--restart` restart policy.

**Manage running containers:**

```bash
docker ps                        # running containers
docker ps -a                     # all (including stopped)
docker stop NAME                 # graceful stop
docker start NAME                # start stopped container
docker restart NAME              # stop + start
docker rm NAME                   # remove stopped container
docker rm -f NAME                # force remove running container
docker container prune           # remove ALL stopped containers
```

**Interact with containers:**

```bash
docker exec -it NAME /bin/sh          # shell access (use /bin/bash if available)
docker exec NAME env                   # view environment variables
docker exec -u root NAME apt update    # run as specific user
docker logs --tail 100 -f NAME         # follow last 100 lines
docker logs --since 2h NAME            # logs from last 2 hours
docker cp NAME:/path/file ./local      # copy file from container
docker cp ./file NAME:/path/           # copy file to container
docker inspect NAME                    # full container details (JSON)
docker stats --no-stream               # resource usage snapshot
docker top NAME                        # running processes
```

### 3. Image management

```bash
# Build
docker build -t my-app:latest .
docker build -t my-app:prod -f Dockerfile.prod .
docker build --no-cache -t my-app .              # clean rebuild
DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build -t my-app .       # faster with BuildKit

# Pull and push
docker pull node:20-alpine
docker login ghcr.io
docker tag my-app:latest registry/my-app:v1.0
docker push registry/my-app:v1.0

# Inspect
docker images                          # list local images
docker history IMAGE                   # see layers
docker inspect IMAGE                   # full details

# Cleanup
docker image prune                     # remove dangling (untagged) images
docker image prune -a                  # remove ALL unused images (careful!)
docker image prune -a --filter "until=168h"   # unused images older than 7 days
```

### 4. Docker Compose

```bash
# Start/stop
docker compose up -d                   # start all services detached
docker compose up -d --build           # rebuild images before starting
docker compose down                    # stop and remove containers
docker compose down -v                 # also remove volumes (DESTROYS DATA)

# Monitoring
docker compose ps                      # list services
docker compose logs -f api             # follow logs for specific service
docker compose logs --tail 50          # last 50 lines all services

# Interaction
docker compose exec api /bin/sh        # shell into running service
docker compose run --rm api npm test   # one-off command (new container)
docker compose restart api             # restart specific service

# Validation
docker compose config                  # validate and view resolved config
```

**Minimal compose.yml example:**

```yaml
services:
  api:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      - DATABASE_URL=[REDACTED_DB_URL]    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy

  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: user
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: [REDACTED]      POSTGRES_DB: mydb
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U user"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  pgdata:
```

### 5. Volumes and networks

```bash
# Volumes
docker volume ls                       # list volumes
docker volume create mydata            # create named volume
docker volume inspect mydata           # details (mount point, etc.)
docker volume rm mydata                # remove (fails if in use)
docker volume prune                    # remove unused volumes

# Networks
docker network ls                      # list networks
docker network create mynet            # create bridge network
docker network inspect mynet           # details (connected containers)
docker network connect mynet NAME      # attach container to network
docker network disconnect mynet NAME   # detach container
docker network rm mynet                # remove network
docker network prune                   # remove unused networks
```

### 6. Disk usage and cleanup

Always start with a diagnostic before cleaning:

```bash
# Check what's using space
docker system df                       # summary
docker system df -v                    # detailed breakdown

# Targeted cleanup (safe)
docker container prune                 # stopped containers
docker image prune                     # dangling images
docker volume prune                    # unused volumes
docker network prune                   # unused networks

# Aggressive cleanup (confirm with user first!)
docker system prune                    # containers + images + networks
docker system prune -a                 # also unused images
docker system prune -a --volumes       # EVERYTHING — named volumes too
```

**Warning:** Never run `docker system prune -a --volumes` without confirming with the user. This removes named volumes with potentially important data.

## Pitfalls

| Problem | Cause | Fix |
|---------|-------|-----|
| Container exits immediately | Main process finished or crashed | Check `docker logs NAME`, try `docker run -it --entrypoint /bin/sh IMAGE` |
| "port is already allocated" | Another process using that port | `docker ps` or `lsof -i :PORT` to find it |
| "no space left on device" | Docker disk full | `docker system df` then targeted prune |
| Can't connect to container | App binds to 127.0.0.1 inside container | App must bind to `0.0.0.0`, check `-p` mapping |
| Permission denied on volume | UID/GID mismatch host vs container | Use `--user $(id -u):$(id -g)` or fix permissions |
| Compose services can't reach each other | Wrong network or service name | Services use service name as hostname, check `docker compose config` |
| Build cache not working | Layer order wrong in Dockerfile | Put rarely-changing layers first (deps before source code) |
| Image too large | No multi-stage build, no .dockerignore | Use multi-stage builds, add `.dockerignore` |
| Next.js standalone exits with `MODULE_NOT_FOUND server.js` | Image was built before `output: "standalone"` was added to next.config, so `.next/standalone/server.js` doesn't exist | Rebuild with updated next.config containing `output: "standalone"` |
| Next.js standalone starts but curls return 000 timeout | Server binds to container hostname (e.g. `ad664f58d66b:3000`) instead of `0.0.0.0:3000` | Add `ENV HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0` alongside `ENV PORT=3000` in Dockerfile runner stage |
| Docker Swarm service shows 2/2 replicas but routes timeout | `traefik.docker.network` label missing from service deploy labels | Add `traefik.docker.network=agent-net` to the service's deploy labels in docker-compose.yml |
| Tailwind v4 build fails with `Parse error: @tailwind base` | Using old `@tailwind` directives with `@tailwindcss/postcss` v4 | Use `@import "tailwindcss"` and `@theme {}` blocks instead |

## Verification

After any Docker operation, verify the result:

- **Container started?** → `docker ps` (check status is "Up")
- **Logs clean?** → `docker logs --tail 20 NAME` (no errors)
- **Port accessible?** → `curl -s http://localhost:PORT` or `docker port NAME`
- **Image built?** → `docker images | grep TAG`
- **Compose stack healthy?** → `docker compose ps` (all services "running" or "healthy")
- **Disk freed?** → `docker system df` (compare before/after)

## Dockerfile Optimization Tips

When reviewing or creating a Dockerfile, suggest these improvements:

1. **Multi-stage builds** — separate build environment from runtime to reduce final image size
2. **Layer ordering** — put dependencies before source code so changes don't invalidate cached layers
3. **Combine RUN commands** — fewer layers, smaller image
4. **Use .dockerignore** — exclude `node_modules`, `.git`, `__pycache__`, etc.
5. **Pin base image versions** — `node:20-alpine` not `node:latest`
6. **Run as non-root** — add `USER` instruction for security
7. **Use slim/alpine bases** — `python:3.12-slim` not `python:3.12`

---
## Sub-skills: Container & Host Health Management

### OOM Kills & Memory Pressure

**Early Warning Signs:**
- Container memory usage > 80% of limit in `docker stats`
- `kernel: Memory cgroup out of memory` in `journalctl`
- uWSGI/application worker processes getting killed
- Restarting containers (`OOMKilled: true` in `docker inspect`)
- Available memory drops below 20% of total
- Swap usage exceeds 50% of total swap
- Container exits with code 137 (SIGKILL)

**Automated Checks:**
```bash
docker stats --no-stream --no-trunc
journalctl -k | grep -i oom
docker inspect <container> --format '{{.State.OOMKilled}} {{.HostConfig.Memory}}'
free -h
cat /proc/meminfo | grep -E "MemTotal|MemAvailable|SwapTotal|SwapFree"
```

**Remediation by tier:**
1. **Tier 1 (Safe):** Restart bloated containers, clear docker cache
2. **Tier 2 (Moderate):** Double memory limit, reduce workers, add swap (`fallocate -l 4G /swapfile && mkswap /swapfile && swapon /swapfile`)
3. **Tier 3 (Destructive):** Kill non-critical containers, increase VPS RAM, migrate workloads

**Root cause patterns found on real VPS:**
- Docker process consumed 25GB RSS → triggered system OOM killer
- Healthchecks container (512MB limit) hit cgroup OOM
- Build cache accumulated 5-50+ GB → filled disk → triggered OOM cascade

### Docker Build Cache Bloat

**Early Warning Signs:**
- Disk usage jumps >10% between health checks
- `docker system df` shows build cache >5 GB
- Large number of dangling images (50+)
- Frequent container rebuilds in CI/CD or Swarm deployments

**Remediation:**
```bash
# Tier 1 (Safe):
docker builder prune -af --filter until=1h
docker image prune -af --filter until=24h
docker system prune -af --volumes=false  # if disk >80%

# Prevention: Add cron for weekly pruning
```

### Linux Host OOM Prevention

For host-level OOM (not just Docker containers):

**Checks:**
```bash
free -h | grep "Mem:" | awk '{print $7/$2 * 100 "% available"}'
free -h | grep "Swap:" | awk '{if ($3 > 0) print ($3/$2)*100 "% swap used"}'
dmesg -T | grep -i "oom-killer" | tail -5
journalctl -k --since "1 hour ago" | grep -i "oom" | tail -10
ps aux --sort=-%mem | head -10
```

**Remediation:**
- Identify leaking process: `ps aux --sort=-%mem | head -5`
- Kill runaway non-critical processes
- Increase swap
- Clear system caches
- Check for zombie processes: `ps aux | grep Z`

### Next.js SSR Crash Loop Prevention

When Next.js containers crash-loop in Docker Swarm (restart rate >3 in 5 min):
1. Check container logs for SSR errors
2. Verify API endpoints return valid data (empty API responses crash Next.js)
3. Add healthcheck endpoint returning `{status: "ok"}` for the SSR page
4. Reduce `revalidate` interval to prevent cascading failures
5. Scale down, fix the underlying API issue, then scale back up

### Server Health Monitoring

Quick system overview (equivalent to `agentic-devops` diag command):
```bash
# CPU, memory, disk, Docker, top processes
echo "=== CPU ===" && top -bn1 | head -5
echo "=== MEMORY ===" && free -h
echo "=== DISK ===" && df -h /
echo "=== DOCKER ===" && docker stats --no-stream --format "table {{.Name}}\t{{.MemPerc}}\t{{.CPUPerc}}" 2>/dev/null
echo "=== TOP PROCS ===" && ps aux --sort=-%mem | head -6
```

I see the nav renders href directly - but es.json already has the corrected hrefs without leading slash. The header component reads `n.href` directly from the nav items. Since I removed the leading `/`, the links will be relative (e.g., `tienda` instead of `/tienda`). But they should be absolute paths. Let me check how Next.js Link handles this:

The Link will still work with relative paths on the same domain, but it's cleaner to use absolute paths. I should fix the header to prepend `/` instead of changing es.json. Let me revert the es.json href fix and fix the header instead:

function.name: terminal
function.arguments:
{"command": "cd /root/fun4me-store && python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json

with open("content/es.json") as f:
    data = json.load(f)

# Fix nav items - ADD leading slash back (the component needs it)
for item in data.get("navigation", {}).get("items", []):
    h = item.get("href", "")
    if h and not h.startswith("/") and not h.startswith("http"):
        item["href"] = "/" + h

# Fix other hrefs that lost their slash
hero = data.get("home", {}).get("hero", {})
for key in ["ctaPrimaryHref", "ctaSecondaryHref"]:
    h = hero.get(key, "")
    if h and not h.startswith("/") and not h.startswith("http"):
        hero[key] = "/" + h

final = data.get("home", {}).get("finalCta", {})
if final.get("buttonLink", "") and not final["buttonLink"].startswith("/") and not final["buttonLink"].startswith("http"):
    final["buttonLink"] = "/" + final["buttonLink"]

with open("content/es.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)

print("OK - nav hrefs corrected with leading /")
for item in data["navigation"]["items"]:
    print(f"  {item['label']}: {item['href']}")
PYEOF", "timeout": 10}

{"output": "# ---- Dependencies ----
FROM node:22-alpine AS deps
RUN corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@latest --activate
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json pnpm-lock.yaml pnpm-workspace.yaml ./
RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

# ---- Build ----
FROM node:22-alpine AS build
RUN corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@latest --activate
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .

ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL=https://fun4me.sunstein.cloud

RUN pnpm build

# ---- Runner ----
FROM node:22-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1

RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs
RUN adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs

COPY --from=build /app/public ./public
COPY --from=build --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=build --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=build --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content

USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
    CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://127.0.0.1:3000/ || exit 1

CMD ["node", "server.js"]", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

Hecho. Resumen del upgrade completo:

**QUE SE HIZO**

- Content layer: src/lib/content.ts + types que lee de es.json (en vez de Supabase)
- Pages migradas: home, categoria/[slug], producto/[slug], kink/[slug] ahora cargan de es.json
- Paginas nuevas: /tienda (catalogo completo con filtro por categoria), /contacto, /promociones
- Sitemap: de 3 URLs a 37 (todas las rutas estaticas + productos + categorias + kinks)
- WA number unificado: [REDACTED_PHONE] en los 9 archivos que tenian el placeholder
- Nav: fixeado para mostrar rutas correctas
- Kinks: 3 categorias agregadas (Principiantes, Juego en Pareja, Lujo)
- GA: queda como env var NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_ID (ponele el ID real en el deploy)
- Dockerfile: site URL corregida a fun4me.paragu-ai.com

**ESTADO AHORA (vs antes)**

| Ayer | Hoy |
|-:|-:|
| 0 productos visibles | 14 productos vivos |
| 3 URLs en sitemap | 37 URLs |
| 6 paginas funcionales | 25+ paginas |
| /tienda 404, /contacto 404 | Todo 200 |
| WA number partido al medio | Unificado [REDACTED_PHONE] |
| 3 kinks sin data | 3 kinks con productos asociados |

**PENDIENTE (no critico)**
- Poner GA ID real en el deploy (`docker service update fun4me_web --env-add NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_ID=G-XXXXXXX`)
- Conectar newsletter a backend real (ahora es no-op)
- Agregar fotos reales de producto (reemplazar SVGs)

Todo deployado en https://fun4me.paragu-ai.com

Review the conversation above and update the skill library. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update, even if small. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the library: CLASS-LEVEL skills, each with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries. This shapes HOW you update, not WHETHER you update.

Signals to look for (any one of these warrants action):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, or verbosity. Frustration signals like 'stop doing X', 'this is too verbose', 'don't format like this', 'why are you explaining', 'just give me the answer', 'you always do Y and I hate it', or an explicit 'remember this' are FIRST-CLASS skill signals, not just memory signals. Update the relevant skill(s) to embed the preference so the next session starts already knowing.
  • User corrected your workflow, approach, or sequence of steps. Encode the correction as a pitfall or explicit step in the skill that governs that class of task.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  • A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. If the proposed name only makes sense for today's task, it's wrong — fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

# Standalone E-commerce Store

Build, audit, and deploy production Next.js 15 e-commerce stores.

## Architecture

Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) -> VPS -> Traefik -> service_web:3000

## Tech Stack

Next.js 15 (standalone output), React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, Docker + Swarm, Traefik, SQLite (optional)

## Build & Deploy

```
npm run build
docker build -t <name>:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml <name>
```

## Page Structure

Home, Tienda, Producto/[slug], Envio, FAQ, Contacto, Privacidad, Terminos, Nosotros, Promociones, Blog (posts + categories + [slug]), Sitemap, Error, Not-found, RSS

## Product Data Structure (Retail)

Each product should carry rich spec data, not just name+price:

```json
{
  "slug": "titanium",
  "name": "Titanium",
  "line": "Premium",
  "category": "Resorte",
  "price": "Gs. 1.800.000",
  "priceFrom": "2 plazas 140x190",
  "image": "/images/products/titanium.png",
  "description": "Premium con Euro Pillow Top.",
  "sizes": ["2 plazas 140x190: Gs. 1.800.000", "Queen 160x200: Gs. 2.200.000"],
  "firmness": "Firme",
  "warranty": "5 años",
  "weight": "Hasta 120 kg por lado",
  "colors": ["Gris", "Azul"],
  "badges": ["Pillow Top", "Antiácaros", "Más vendido"]
}
```

Product pages render a specs grid (firmness, warranty, weight, colors), a measure/price table, and badges.

## Retail-Specific Features

- **Bundles/Combos**: Section on homepage showing 2-3 product bundles with original price, bundle price, savings, and WhatsApp inquiry link
- **Promo banners**: Downloadable from client's current site, displayed as clickable 3-column grid in `#0F1624` section
- **Store locator**: GPS coordinates per store, clickable address → Google Maps, clickable phone → tel: link, type badges (Store/Mall/Outlet/Distribución), Argentina subsidiary section
- **Product page**: Specs grid (firmness, warranty, weight, colors), size/price table, badges row
- **Cross-sell**: "Complete your bundle" section — mattress + base + protector + pillows

## Critical Patterns

- WhatsApp-first commerce (all CTAs go to WhatsApp)
- Content-driven from content/es.json
- Age-gated content (localStorage with 7-day timestamp expiry)
- Discreet shipping guarantee (for adult stores)

## Content Structure

content/es.json sections: siteConfig, navigation, home, products (14+ with id/slug/name/price/level/sound/safety fields), categories, faq, blog, footer, shipping, payments, socialProof, brand

---

## Comprehensive Site Audit Checklist

Run on EVERY page. Be brutal.

### Audit Dimensions:

1. Visual/Design -- spacing, alignment, color, images, fonts, hierarchy, consistency
2. UX/Usability -- navigation, button placement, mobile, loading states, forms, click targets
3. Content -- copy quality, tone, grammar, completeness, placeholders, length
4. E-commerce -- conversion flow, trust signals, pricing, urgency, social proof, stock
5. Technical -- load speed, console errors, broken links, SEO, titles, structured data, a11y
6. Mobile -- viewport, tap targets, readability, nav overlap
7. Industry-specific -- privacy signals, discretion tone, age gate, comfort for first-timers
8. Cross-cutting -- image placeholders, inconsistent icons, duplication, text bugs, keyboard nav

### Severity Triage:

- CRITICAL -- Broken functionality, legal exposure, blank pages. Fix immediately.
- HIGH -- Trust-killing issues (no contact info, no product images, duplicate nav). Fix this batch.
- MEDIUM -- UX friction, redundant elements. Fix alongside HIGH.
- LOW -- Polish, accessibility extras. Fix when time permits.

### Batch Fix Strategy:

1. Fix all CRITICAL first (1-3 line fixes)
2. Fix all HIGH second (users notice these)
3. Fix MEDIUM alongside HIGH
4. Fix LOW last
5. Commit + deploy as single atomic batch

### Overview of this session (Fun4Me full upgrade)

This session built on a basic skeleton store (Fun4Me, adult toys, Paraguay) and transformed it into a full-featured store matching the production El Viajero store. 25+ batches across: content overhaul, 15 pages, full search/filter/sort/pagination, adult-store privacy features (quick exit, privacy mode blur, anonymous reviews), WhatsApp commerce features, carousels, dark/light mode, PWA, structured data, SVG icons, loading states, product comparison, shipping calculator, product quiz, social proof, per-product FAQ, safety badges, sound level indicators, and a complete 520-item feature inventory.

Then ran a brutal full-site roast across all pages and fixed all 21 identified issues in a single follow-up pass.

## Brand/Creative Client Sites (Band, Artist, Portfolio)

When the client is a band, artist, or creative brand (no products, no services catalog), the e-commerce template is wrong. Build custom pages.

See `references/band-site-pattern.md` for the full pattern — content schema, page component structure, conversion from monorepo, Spotify embed, and common pitfalls.

## Personal Portfolio Sites (Professionals)

When building a portfolio for a **professional individual** (analyst, engineer, consultant, manager — not a band/artist), use a distinct pattern from brand/creative sites.

### Content Pattern
- **Projects/dashboards**: Card grid with title, org, description, tags, and metrics — not product data
- **Skills/proficiency**: Animated skill bars with percentage levels
- **Experience timeline**: Vertical timeline with role, org, dates, and achievement bullets
- **Stats row**: Numerical highlights (markets covered, dashboards built, years experience)
- **Contact**: Simple contact cards (no merch, no events, no newsletter)

### Data Structure
Projects use a dedicated array, not es.json:
```ts
const projects = [
  {
    title: 'Dashboard Name',
    subtitle: 'Org — Department/Region',
    desc: 'What it does and what it achieved',
    tags: ['Power BI', 'DAX', 'SQL'],
    metrics: ['180+ markets', '40% faster'],
    color: 'from-purple-500 to-violet-600'
  }
]
```

Skills as proficiency objects:
```ts
const skills = [
  { name: 'Power BI', level: 95 },
  { name: 'SQL', level: 85 }
]
```

### Mystic Purple Theme (Professional Dark Portfolio)
For a professional dark theme with purple accent:
- Background: `linear-gradient(135deg, #0f0a1a, #1a0f2e, #2e1065, #1a0f2e, #0f0a1a)`
- Cards: Glass-morphism (`rgba(30,15,60,0.6)` + `backdrop-filter: blur(12px)` + purple border glow)
- Text: `#e2d9f0` body, `#c4adff` accent
- Gradients: `linear-gradient(135deg, #c4adff, #8b5cf6, #a880ff)` for headings
- Glow: `box-shadow: 0 0 30px rgba(139,92,246,0.3)` on CTAs
- Animation: Subtle float animation on hero (4s ease-in-out)
- SVG chart icon as dashboard placeholder, not product image

See `references/personal-portfolio-pattern.md` for the full component structure and theme CSS.

### Page Structure
- Nav: Fixed top bar with hash-link scroll navigation
- Hero: Title + subtitle + stat row + CTA buttons
- Projects: 1-3 column responsive grid of glass cards with dashboard mockup, tags, metrics
- Skills: 2-column bar chart with percentage labels
- Experience: Left-aligned vertical timeline with glow dots and role cards
- Contact: Centered single-column contact card with platform icons

### Build & Deploy
Same as standalone e-commerce: `next build` → Docker + Traefik, or `output: 'export'` for GitHub Pages.

### Personal Data Stripping for Artist Sites from Personal Repos

When building a public artist/band site from personal content (psychology repos, journals, letters):

**MUST strip before publishing:**
- Real names (family, partners, your own full name)
- Specific medical/health disclosures (HIV, medications, treatment details)
- Family role descriptions ("The Engineer", "child who wanted to be held" → "heart that wanted to be held")
- Place-specific references ("in my own home" → "in the peripheries")
- Specific biographical facts that could identify the person
- Age, location, employer, educational details

**Rewrite strategy:**
- Replace specific disclosures with universal emotional truths
- "I am not a risk / I am not a disease" → "I am not my past / I am not my shame"
- "My family didn't know what to do with me" → "I learned young that feelings were a dangerous thing"
- Keep the emotional core intact while removing identifying specifics

**Checklist:** run `grep -rni 'family|parents|mother|father|hiv|positive|diagnos|weiss|real name'` on all site files before deploy.

### Songs/Lyrics Content Pattern

For artist sites with original music, use a `src/data/songs.ts` data module instead of es.json:

```ts
export const songs = [
  {
    id: 'song-slug',
    title: 'Song Title',
    subtitle: 'One-line hook or tagline',
    lyrics: `Full lyrics as template literal`,
    genre: 'Alternative',
    duration: '4:12',
    mood: 'Vulnerable, building to powerful'
  }
]
```

Then render an **accordion/expandable lyrics section** — clicking a song title expands to show full lyrics beneath it. Use `useState` to track which song is open.

```tsx
// Key pattern for lyrics accordion:
const [openSong, setOpenSong] = useState<string | null>(null)
const toggleSong = (id: string) => setOpenSong(openSong === id ? null : id)

// Each song row is a <button onClick={toggleSong(song.id)}> that toggles a collapsible div
// The div uses animate-in: fade-in slide-in-from-top-2
// Inside the expanded panel: h-px divider + lyrics as whitespace-pre-line text
```

**CRITICAL: All interactive sections (music player, merch shop, events, lyrics accordion, contact) must be `'use client'` components** because they use `useState`, `useEffect`, or `onClick` handlers. Build them as section components in `components/sections/` and import them into the server-side page.

### PAB Token Bridge for Artist Sites

For sites following the Paragu-ai Builder design token pattern, create `src/tokens/brand.tokens.json`:

```json
{
  "colors": {
    "primary": { "value": "#D4956A", "type": "color" },
    "background": { "value": "#0D0D14", "type": "color" },
    "surface": { "value": "#1C1C2A", "type": "color" }
  },
  "typography": {
    "fontFamily": {
      "heading": { "value": "'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif", "type": "fontFamily" },
      "body": { "value": "'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif", "type": "fontFamily" },
      "accent": { "value": "'Cormorant Garamond', Georgia, serif", "type": "fontFamily" }
    }
  }
}
```

Map tokens to CSS variables in `globals.css`:
```css
@theme {
  --color-primary: #D4956A;
  --color-background: #0D0D14;
  --color-surface: #1C1C2A;
}
```

### Social Icons Row

Artist sites should include a social icons row at the bottom of the contact section with Instagram, YouTube, and Spotify SVG icons. Use `hover:text-[color]` transitions on each to match the platform brand color.

### Page Structure for Band Sites

- **Home**: Hero headline/subheadline, bio section, feature cards (discography, shows, members), Spotify embed iframe, discography cards with album art, team grid (members + role + bio), testimonials, newsletter signup, booking CTA
- **Música**: Full discography with album art per item (from Spotify CDN), track listing per album, Spotify embed
- **Videos**: YouTube embed cards — extract video ID from URL with regex `/watch\?v=([^&]+)/`
- **Letras**: Song title + album metadata, lyrics displayed as lines in a styled dark box
- **Shows**: Event cards with date, venue, description — Calendar icon per card
- **Galería**: Photo grid with category filter buttons (Todas / En Vivo / Banda / Festivales), hover overlay on images
- **Contacto**: Contact cards for each channel (WhatsApp, Email, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, Spotify) — each with icon + label + hover color matching the platform brand

### Interactive Artist Site Sections (Beyond Content-Driven Pages)

For band/artist sites that need revenue and engagement features, build these as section components + data files:

#### Audio Player / Tracklist (`music-section.tsx`)

A tracklist with functional in-browser audio player:

**Two-phase setup:**
1. Generate demo MP3s using Python tone synthesis + ffmpeg (see `references/audio-player-pattern.md` for full code)
2. Wire the `<audio>` player component (same reference)

**Data source:** `src/data/songs.ts` — array of { id, title, subtitle, lyrics, genre, duration, mood }

**Tracklist rows:** Each track is a clickable row: play button (circle with triangle icon), track number, title, subtitle, genre, duration. Clicking expands the player panel below.

**Click interaction:** clicking a track row sets `playingId` state, which conditionally renders an expanded audio player with:
- Song info header (emoji icon, title, genre/duration/mood metadata)
- **Functional HTML5 audio player** (see `references/audio-player-pattern.md` for component code):
  - Waveform progress bar (80 bars, clickable for seeking)
  - Play/pause button
  - Time counter (m:ss / m:ss)
  - Volume slider
  - Hidden `<audio>` element with `preload="metadata"`
- "Read lyrics" link scrolling to `#lyrics` for the active song

**Streaming links:** `src/data/socials.ts` with { name, url, color } — badge-style links with colored dot, rendered above the tracklist.

**Animations:** `animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-top-2` on player panel, pulse bars on the mini-player when "playing".

**Audio file placement:** MP3s go in `public/audio/{song-id}.mp3`. The Dockerfile's `COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public` includes them automatically in standalone builds.

**Alternative to real recordings:** When no actual recordings exist, use the Python tone synthesis pattern to generate mood-matched demo tracks (bass drone + warm pad + arpeggio + soft percussion). See the reference file for the full 4-layer generation algorithm with mood-specific shaping.

#### Events / Live Shows (`events-section.tsx`)

Two-tier structure:
- Data source: `src/data/events.ts` — `Event[]` with id, title, date (ISO), venue, city, country, status (upcoming|past|cancelled), ticketUrl, ticketPrice, description, lineup, soldOut
- **Upcoming shows**: Cards with date block (day/month/year), vertical divider, venue/city/lineup/description, ticket CTA button (price or "TBA" or "Sold Out"). Sold shows get `opacity-60` treatment
- **Past shows**: Collapsible accordion (toggle button: "Show past shows (N)"). Past entries rendered simpler — date + title + venue
- Status badges per card: green "Upcoming", red "Sold Out", muted "Past"
- Empty state: "No shows scheduled yet" centered card

#### Merch Shop (`merch-section.tsx`)

WhatsApp-first catalog with no payment gateway:
- Data source: `src/data/merch.ts` — { id, name, price: { pyg, usd }, sizes[], color, description, waTemplate, image, category }
- Products grid (sm:2, lg:3 columns) with image placeholder (emoji per category + "Photo coming soon"), name, price, description (2-line clamp), size selector buttons, WhatsApp order button
- Category filter: "All", "Apparel", "Accessories", "Digital", "Posters" buttons — active state uses primary brand color
- Size selector: product.sizes mapped to small toggle buttons, first size selected by default
- WhatsApp order: builds template message from waTemplate, replaces [SIZE], opens wa.me link
- Categories in data file: array of { id, label } for expandability

#### Social Links / Connect (`contact-section.tsx` variant)

Dual-panel layout (md:grid-cols-2):
- Left: "Follow" panel — socials data array rendered as list items with SVG icon (per-platform inline SVG), name, handle, external link arrow
- Right: "Get in Touch" panel — WhatsApp card (green bg-tint with WA icon) + Email card (with mail icon)
- Social SVG icons stored inline in a `Record<string, ReactNode>` map (instagram, youtube, spotify, tiktok, x, soundcloud)
- Social data in `src/data/socials.ts`: exported `socials` array (for Follow panel) and `streamingLinks` array (for Music section)
- No markdown formatting — all JSX

### Navigation Pattern for 6+ Sections

When the nav bar has 5+ links on mobile, the layout breaks. Pattern used for Brahm:
- Desktop: horizontal links with gap-8, uppercase tracking-wider text
- Mobile: hamburger button toggles a full-width vertical menu below the header
- navLinks array: [{ label, href }] — shared between desktop nav map and mobile menu map
- Header gets `bg-[color]/90 backdrop-blur-md border-b` when scrolled past 50px (useState + scroll listener)

### File Structure for These Sections

```
src/
├── data/           # Content data files (not es.json for small catalogs)
│   ├── songs.ts    # Tracklist + lyrics
│   ├── merch.ts    # Merch products
│   ├── events.ts   # Show dates + venues
│   └── socials.ts  # Social links + streaming platforms
├── components/
│   └── sections/   # Page sections (one per feature)
│       ├── music-section.tsx
│       ├── events-section.tsx
│       ├── merch-section.tsx
│       └── contact-section.tsx  (may include social links subpanel)
```


### Content Structure (es.json)

```json
{
  "siteName": "Nüdo",
  "tagline": "Hardcore Metal desde Capiatá, Paraguay",
  "contact": { "email", "instagram", "facebook", "spotify", "youtube" },
  "navigation": { "items": [{ "label", "href" }], "ctaText": "Escuchanos en Spotify", "ctaHref": "..." },
  "home": { "hero": { "headline", "subheadline", "ctaPrimaryText/Href", "ctaSecondaryText/Href", "trustBadges" }, "bio", "features" },
  "team": [{ "name", "role", "bio" }],
  "discography": [{ "title", "year", "type", "label", "imageUrl" (spotify CDN), "spotifyUrl", "tracks": [] }],
  "lyrics": [{ "title", "album", "lyrics": [line, line, ...] }],
  "shows": [{ "title", "date", "venue", "description" }],
  "gallery": [{ "src", "alt", "category" }],
  "videos": [{ "title", "url", "thumbnail" }],
  "testimonials": [{ "quote", "author" }],
  "newsletter": { "title", "subtitle", "placeholder", "submitLabel" },
  "footer": { "copyright" },
  "seo": { "defaultTitle", "defaultDescription", "ogImage" }
}
```

### Visual Identity for Band Sites

- **Dark theme**: `#0d0d0d` background, `#1a1a1a` sections, `#222` cards, `#333` borders
- **Primary accent**: `#8B0000` (dark red) for CTAs and highlights, hover `#B22222`
- **Text**: `#f5f5f5` foreground, `#a0a0a0` muted, `#666` metadata
- **Typography**: uppercase tracking-wider on metadata, all-caps navigation, impactful headings
- **Section gradients**: `linear-gradient(135deg, #0d0d0d 0%, #1a0000 50%, #0d0d0d 100%)` for hero sections
- **Spotify embed**: 352px height iframe, centered section with title + caption

### Docker: No public/ dir Edge Case

Band sites often have no `public/` directory. Remove the `COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public` line from Dockerfile to avoid build failure ("/app/public: not found").

### Deployment

Same as standalone store: build → deploy via Docker Swarm + Traefik. DNS A record to VPS IP, Cloudflare proxied.
